claim: Premium studio homepages follow a consistent 5-section sequence: Hero, Process, Work, About, Contact
GSAP + Lenis + ScrollTrigger is the dominant animation stack for premium studio sites (60%+ of award-winning sites)
Variable fonts have become the standard choice for premium studio homepages in 2026
GSAP SplitText with scroll-triggered character/word reveals has replaced CSS keyframe animations for premium text animation
Hero sections use 5 dominant patterns: full-screen video, split-screen, typography-only, abstract/3D, and product demo
Typography-only heroes are growing fastest in 2026 as typography-only pattern usage increases
Approximately 65% of creative studio homepages use hamburger menus on desktop
Studio homepages use remarkably little text — typical headlines are 3-7 words
The bento-grid asymmetrical layout has overtaken traditional card grids for portfolio sections
Top studios ship ≤300KB CSS, ≤400KB JS, and keep LCP under 1.8s despite heavy animation
Webflow is used by ~35% of studio sites, Framer ~25%, Next.js ~30%, SvelteKit ~10%
Page transitions have moved from enhancement to expected feature via Barba.js, Swup, or View Transitions API
Footers on premium studio homepages have evolved into a final brand statement
GSAP went free (MIT-licensed) in 2025 after Webflow's acquisition
CSS handles ~80% of all animation use cases with zero bundle cost
The aesthetic-usability effect shows users perceive more attractive designs as easier to use
Hero videos should be ≤4MB desktop, ≤2MB mobile, H.264 MP4 with muted autoplay loop playsinline
The view transitions API (Chrome 115+, Safari 17.4+) is a growing native browser alternative for page transitions
Studio homepages serve simultaneously as portfolio and proof-of-capability
Continuous horizontal marquee is used by ~25% of studio portfolio sections
